history.title: Messier catalogues M34
history.summary: Charles Messier records a loose cluster of small stars in Perseus — one of the nearest open clusters visible in binoculars.
history.detail: NGC 1039 sits at roughly 1,500 light-years. Its age and distance would remain uncertain for two centuries.

history.title: Multicolor surveys
history.summary: Photographic and photoelectric campaigns map the main sequence and establish reddening and distance.
history.detail: Canterna et al. (1979) and Jones & Prosser (1996) provide membership via proper motions to V ≈ 16.

history.title: WIYN & Monitor projects
history.summary: Time-series photometry reveals rotation periods and the color–period diagram.
history.detail: Meibom et al. and Barnes et al. derive a gyrochronology age near 193 Myr from spotted rotators.

history.title: Astrometric revolution
history.summary: Gaia DR2/DR3 parallaxes and proper motions redefine cluster membership at scale.
history.detail: Cantat-Gaudin & Anders (2020) publish probabilistic member lists for M34 among 1,400+ clusters.

history.title: Multi-band binary census
history.summary: Infrared-sensitive diagrams detect unresolved companions missed by B–V alone.
history.detail: Malofeeva et al. (2023) estimate binary fractions of 45–73% in the intermediate-mass range.

history.title: Project Midas
history.summary: Isochrone–binary diagnostics on deep BVR(I) photometry.
history.detail: Excel and Python pipelines classify hundreds of candidate single and binary main-sequence stars.

history.title: Revival & validation
history.summary: Cross-matching legacy photometry with Gaia, TESS, and modern binary catalogs.
history.detail: Open questions: binary completeness, white dwarf membership, and method comparison.
